sequentialTasks.task: T04a: REARCHITECT sendToHandle as async Promise-based function
sequentialTasks.description: The blocking time.Sleep approach (even in a spawned goroutine with select) VIOLATES the event loop contract. Go function calls from JS block the event loop until they return. CORRECT ARCHITECTURE: (1) Remove sendTextWithEnter Go function entirely. (2) In JS, make sendToHandle return a Promise. (3) Use setTimeout for the 10ms delay: return new Promise(resolve => { sendWithCancel(text); setTimeout(() => { sendWithCancel(newline); resolve({error:null}); }, 10); }). (4) Update ALL callers of sendToHandle to use await or .then() pattern. (5) This requires converting affected functions to async. Files: pr_split_script.js (sendToHandle, sendClassificationPrompt, etc.), pr_split.go (remove sendTextWithEnter binding).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: sendToHandle returns Promise. Delay uses JS setTimeout (not Go time.Sleep). Event loop never blocked. All callers use await. Tests verify async behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: T09: Regression replication — force-kill leaves orphaned descendants
sequentialTasks.description: BEFORE fixing PTY process groups, write a test that: (1) Spawns a PTY process (via internal/builtin/pty Spawn) that in turn creates a child subprocess (e.g., shell running 'sleep 3600' or a test helper that holds a lock file). (2) Sends SIGKILL via Process.Signal('SIGKILL'). (3) Asserts the child subprocess PID STILL EXISTS after the parent dies — proving orphan survival. Use deterministic PID checking (syscall.Kill(pid, 0)) with cleanup to prevent test pollution. Name it TestPTYSpawn_ForceKill_OrphanSurvival.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test demonstrates child process survives parent SIGKILL — proving the force-kill orphan bug exists in current code.

sequentialTasks.task: T10: Fix PTY spawn to use process groups (Setpgid)
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/builtin/pty/pty_unix.go, function Spawn (search 'func Spawn'): (1) Before cmd.Start(), set `cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. This creates a new process group for the child. (2) In unixProcessHandle.Signal() (search 'func (h *unixProcessHandle) Signal'), change from `h.cmd.Process.Signal(sig)` (which sends to PID) to `syscall.Kill(-h.cmd.Process.Pid, sig.(syscall.Signal))` (which sends to process GROUP via negative PID). (3) Handle the type assertion for non-syscall.Signal types gracefully. (4) This change is Unix-only (pty_unix.go), so Windows builds are unaffected.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: SIGKILL kills entire process group. TestPTYSpawn_ForceKill_OrphanSurvival now passes (orphan no longer survives). Clean SIGTERM shutdown still works. If sig type assertion to syscall.Signal fails, fall back to os.Process.Signal() for the individual PID — log a warning but do not panic.

continuousVerification.task: After EVERY significant change, run make pipeline and integration tests
continuousVerification.description: Run `make make-all-with-log` after every code change. Run `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` after pr-split changes.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix. Zero tolerance for flaky tests.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: Do not claim a regression is fixed without test evidence.
No commits until two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews confirm correctness.
